Add-on Treatment.evidence:
Title: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer"
- Abstract: "Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) were shown in the CHAARTED trial in patients with metastatic high-volume disease (HVD)."
- Patients and Methods: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m2 for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Docetaxel. This is explicitly stated in the title of the paper on page 1: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone". It is also mentioned in the abstract on page 1: "Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)". Further details are in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2, stating "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The document mentions causes of all deaths (disease progression, other causes, unknown) but does not provide a breakdown of all-cause Grade 3 or higher adverse events for the treatment arm.

Not reported. The document mentions causes of all deaths (disease progression, other causes, unknown) but does not provide a breakdown of all-cause Grade 3 or higher adverse events for the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The document only specifies "treatment-related deaths" (Grade 5) but does not provide a count or percentage for all treatment-related adverse events of Grade 3 or higher.
Not reported. The document does not provide a count or percentage for treatment-related adverse events of Grade 3 or higher in the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm including two neutropenia-related deaths (both in the HVD subgroup). The number of patients in the ADT plus D arm is 192 (from Table 1 on page 3). Calculation: 4/192 = 0.0208, or approximately 2.1%
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Locate the sentence in section 3.1, "Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm including two neutropenia-related deaths (both in the HVD subgroup)." The number of patients in the ADT plus D arm is 192 (from Table 1 on page 3).

Not reported. The document explicitly states that "Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm," implying no such deaths were reported for the ADT alone (control) arm. However, it does not explicitly state "0" or "none" for the control arm.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: This column refers to the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence in randomized controlled trials (RCTs), which is typically performed as part of a systematic review or meta-analysis to assess the quality or risk of bias of included studies. This document is a primary research article reporting the results of an RCT and does not contain such an external assessment or a self-assessment using a standardized risk judgment framework for its own evidence criteria. While the paper discusses its limitations (e.g., lack of statistical power, retrospective analysis), these are not presented as an "overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)".

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: Page 1: "The role of chemotherapy in metastatic non castrate prostate cancer (mNCPC) is debated. Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) were shown in the CHAARTED trial in patients with metastatic high-volume disease (HVD)." Page 1 ("Patient summary"): "However, early chemotherapy should be discussed with all patients, given the data of three randomized trials including GETUG-AFU15."
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The class of agent for Docetaxel (D), which is part of Treatment Arm 1 (ADT plus D), is chemotherapy. This is explicitly stated and implied throughout the text. For example, on page 1, in the abstract, it mentions 'Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) were shown in the CHAARTED trial in patients with metastatic high-volume disease (HVD).' and 'The role of chemotherapy in metastatic non castrate prostate cancer (mNCPC) is debated.' On page 2, in the 'Patient summary', it states 'early chemotherapy should be discussed with all patients...'.

Control Arm.evidence: Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr.
Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm is "ADT alone" (or simply "ADT"). This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". It is also referred to as "ADT" in Table 1 on page 3 and Table 2 on page 4, and in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

Control Arm - N.evidence: From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.
Control Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the control arm (ADT arm) is 193. This information is reported in the text on page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section: "From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm." It is also confirmed in Table 1 on page 3, under the column "ADT (n = 193)".

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The number of patients randomized to the ADT plus D arm was 192 (page 2, text). The text also states that "patients received a median number of eight and six cycles of D" (page 5, text), implying actual administration. However, an explicit count and percentage of participants in the treatment arm who *received* docetaxel (e.g., "X patients (Y%) received docetaxel") is not reported.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179) indicating that this trial was more a trial of early versus delayed D. The GETUG-AFU15 trial randomized 385 patients including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. (Calculation: 0.79 × 193 = 152.47, rounded to 152)
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: In the control arm (ADT arm), 152 patients (79%) received docetaxel as salvage therapy. Locate the paragraph on page 5 that states: "In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179) indicating that this trial was more a trial of early versus delayed D." The number of patients in the ADT arm is 193 (page 2, text). Calculate 79% of 193 to get the count (0.79 * 193 = 152.47, rounded to 152).

Median Age (years) | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median age for the treatment arm (ADT plus D) is reported as 63.0 years in Table 1, under the row "Age, yr, median (IQR)" and column "ADT plus D (n = 192)".

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).plan_extraction_plan: Extract the value "83.9" from the "Median follow-up, mo" row under the "GETUG-AFU 15" column in Table 2.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: In the overall population, the median OS was not significantly different between the two arms: 62.1 mo (95% CI, 49.5–73.7) in the ADT plus D arm and 48.6 mo (95% CI, 40.9–60.6) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.88 [95% CI, 0.68–1.14]; p = 0.3) (Fig. 1, Table 2).
Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median OS for the overall population in the ADT plus D arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "All patients" column, row "ADT plus D, mo". The value is 62.1.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: In the overall population, the median OS was not significantly different between the two arms: 62.1 mo (95% CI, 49.5–73.7) in the ADT plus D arm and 48.6 mo (95% CI, 40.9–60.6) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.88 [95% CI, 0.68–1.14]; p = 0.3) (Fig. 1, Table 2).
Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The median OS for the overall population in the ADT arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "All patients" column, row "ADT, mo". The value is 48.6.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm, but the difference was not statistically significant: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14) (Fig. 2).
Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median OS for high-volume disease (HVD) patients in the ADT plus D arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "HVD" column, row "ADT plus D, mo". The value is 39.8.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm, but the difference was not statistically significant: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14) (Fig. 2).
Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The median OS for high-volume disease (HVD) patients in the ADT arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "HVD" column, row "ADT, mo". The value is 35.1.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: In patients with LVD, median OS was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8–NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67–1.55]; p = 0.9) (Fig. 3).
Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median OS for low-volume disease (LVD) patients in the ADT plus D arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "LVD" column, row "ADT plus D, mo". The value is NR (Not Reached).

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: In patients with LVD, median OS was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8–NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67–1.55]; p = 0.9) (Fig. 3).
Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The median OS for low-volume disease (LVD) patients in the ADT arm is reported in Table 2 on page 4, under the "GETUG-AFU 15" section, "LVD" column, row "ADT, mo". The value is 83.4.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: Discrepancies between the GETUG-AFU15 and the CHAARTED studies cannot be explained by treatment exposure because patients received a median number of eight and six cycles of D, respectively." ... "Docetaxel (D) ... 75 mg/m2 for 21 d, up to nine cycles.
Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.plan_extraction_plan: The median number of docetaxel (D) cycles for patients in the GETUG-AFU15 trial's ADT plus D arm was 8 cycles. Docetaxel was administered in 21-day cycles. Calculate the total duration: 8 cycles * 21 days/cycle = 168 days. Convert days to months (assuming 30.4375 days/month for an average month): 168 days / 30.4375 days/month ≈ 5.5 months.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm received Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) alone, which is described as continuous therapy. The document does not report a specific median finite duration in months for this continuous on-treatment therapy. While median overall survival (48.6 months) and biochemical progression-free survival (12.9 months) for the ADT arm are reported in Table 2 on page 4, these are not explicitly stated as 'median on-treatment duration' for the continuous ADT regimen.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5–28.4) versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) (HR: 0.67 [95% CI, 0.54–0.84]; p < 0.001)
Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the overall population in the ADT plus D arm is reported as 22.9 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5–28.4) versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) (HR: 0.67 [95% CI, 0.54–0.84]; p < 0.001)

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for the LVD group in the ADT plus D arm is reported as 40.9 months in the "Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival" subsection of the Results on page 4, first paragraph.

The value for the ADT plus D arm is 115.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr.
Number of Arms Included.plan_extraction_plan: The number of arms included is 2. This is reported in the text on page 1, in the abstract under "Design, setting, and participants", stating "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone". It is also explicitly stated in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "The GETUG-AFU15 trial is a French multicenter open-label randomized study [13]. From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm."

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The document reports median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in months for the ADT plus D (treatment) arm, but not a percentage rate (e.g., 1-year PFS rate). Specifically, median bPFS was 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5–28.4) and median rPFS was 22.9 mo (95% CI, 20.5-31.4) for the ADT plus D arm. The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84) and for rPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87). However, a direct PFS rate as a percentage is not reported.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The document reports median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in months for the ADT (control) arm, but not a percentage rate (e.g., 1-year PFS rate). Specifically, median bPFS was 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7) and median rPFS was 15.3 mo (95% CI, 12.4-19.8) for the ADT arm. The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84) and for rPFS (ADT plus D vs ADT) was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87). However, a direct PFS rate as a percentage is not reported.

Quality of Life Scale.plan_extraction_plan: The "Quality of Life Scale" is not reported as an outcome for the GETUG-AFU15 trial in this PDF. The primary and secondary endpoints for the GETUG-AFU15 trial are listed on page 2 under "Outcome measurements and statistical analysis" and include overall survival (OS), biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS), and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS), but not quality of life. While "quality of life" is mentioned on page 6 (References, [26] and [27]) in the context of a *different* trial (GETUG 12), it is not an outcome reported for the GETUG-AFU15 study.

Quality of Life reported.plan_extraction_plan: The clinical trial (GETUG-AFU15) does not report results on the quality of life of participants in this PDF. While "quality of life" is mentioned in the titles of references [26] and [27] on page 6, these refer to the GETUG 12 trial, not the GETUG-AFU15 trial which is the subject of this paper. The main text, abstract, and results sections of the paper do not include any data or discussion regarding quality of life for the GETUG-AFU15 study.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6–NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5–54.6)" and "In our study, this represented 28% of cases, and these patients had a significantly longer survival (83.1 vs 46.5 mo; HR: 1.57 [95% CI, 1.09–2.26]; p = 0.015). But no significant difference in OS was observed between treatment arms (ADT plus D vs ADT) between patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases." ("Metastases at diagnosis" and "de novo" refer to synchronous presentation.)
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with synchronous metastases. On page 3, section 3.1, under "Overall survival", the text states: "Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6-NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5-54.6)". This indicates that "metastases at diagnosis" (synchronous) is a reported prognostic group. Further confirmation is on page 5, section 4, which states: "But no significant difference in OS was observed between treatment arms (ADT plus D vs ADT) between patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases." "De novo" refers to synchronous.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6–NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5–54.6)" and "But no significant difference in OS was observed between treatment arms (ADT plus D vs ADT) between patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases." ("Metastatic disease after failure of local treatment" and "secondary metastatic diseases" refer to metachronous presentation.)
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with metachronous metastases. On page 3, section 3.1, under "Overall survival", the text states: "Patients with metastatic disease after failure of local treatment (28%) had a significantly longer median OS than those with metastases at diagnosis (72%) at 83.1 mo (95% CI, 68.6-NR) versus 46.5 mo (95% CI, 40.5-54.6)". "Metastatic disease after failure of local treatment" refers to metachronous metastases. Further confirmation is on page 5, section 4, which states: "But no significant difference in OS was observed between treatment arms (ADT plus D vs ADT) between patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases." "Secondary metastatic diseases" refers to metachronous.

We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups." and "In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm, but the difference was not statistically significant: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6)

We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups." HVD stands for high-volume disease.
